person.name: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger

nameForms.fullText: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger

km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger於大約1195年出生在英國。他與Avelina de Courtena結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger于大约1195年出生在英国。他与Avelina de Courtena结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel?
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger was born about 1195, in England. He married Avelina de Courtena. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger est né environ 1195, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Avelina de Courtena. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger wurde um 1195 in England geboren. Er hat Avelina de Courtena geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger è nato il circa 1195 nel England. Lui sposato Avelina de Courtena. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Legerは約1195年にイギリスで生まれた。Avelina de Courtenaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger(은)는 대략 1195년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 Avelina de Courtena(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger nasceu aproximadamente 1195, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Avelina de Courtena. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger родился около 1195 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Avelina de Courtena. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Husband Of Isabel? De Saint Leger nació aproximadamente 1195, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Avelina de Courtena. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
